memberdef.argsstring: (radiat, temp, suncos, f0, hstar, xmw, ustar, cfrac, pressu, xlai, ii, spc, salinity, tskin, iodide, xlon, ylat, co2_effect, co2_level, co2_ref, rsurfc, rc)
memberdef.name: wesely_rc_gas

briefdescription.para: Computes the bulk surface resistance (Rc) for the gas species.

detaileddescription.para: References: Wesely, M. L. "Parameterization of surface resistances to gaseous dry deposition in regional-scale numerical models." Atmospheric environment 41 (2007): 52-63. https://doi.org/10.1016/0004-6981(89)90153-4

parameterdescription.para: React. factor for oxidation depends on species
Henry's law constant depends on species
Index of the drydep land type
React. factor for oxidation depends on species (inout because it is changed in the function)
Henry's law constant depends on species
Index of the drydep land type

briefdescription.para: calculates the dry deposition velocity of O3 to ocean

detaileddescription.para: References: Pound, R. J., Sherwen, T., Helmig, D., Carpenter, L. J., and Evans, M. J.: Influence of oceanic ozone deposition on tropospheric photochemistry, Atmos. Chem. Phys., https://doi.org/10.5194/acp-20-4227-2020, 2020.

parameterdescription.para: output of the new deposition vel [cm/s]

briefdescription.para: estimate the modified Bessel functions of the second order zero (K0) and one (K1).

detaileddescription.para: References: Approach initially described in Numerical Recipes in Fortran 90 second edition (1996). This implementation is designed to be specific to the use case required for calculating oceanic deposition velocity. Uses a polynomial fit of each type of modified bessel function to estimate the value of the function for each input.

parameterdescription.para: !the value we want the soln for
output of the modified bessel functions

briefdescription.para: calculate the value of a polynomial fit used in the K0K1_APPROX function in estimating the values of a modified bessel function.
calculates the molecular diffusivity [m2/s] in air for a gas X of molecular weight XM [kg] at temperature TK [K] and pressure PRESS [Pa].
computes the light correction used in the dry deposition and canopy NOx modules.

detaileddescription.para: It was part of the old Harvard-GISS CTM and was ported into GEOS-Chem
References: Wang, Y., D.J. Jacob, and J.A. Logan, "Global simulation of tropospheric O3-NOx-hydrocarbon chemistry, 1. Model formulation", J. Geophys. Res., 103/D9, 10,713-10,726, 1998.

memberdef.argsstring: (temp, pressu, xmw, ustar, obk, zo, thik, is_gas, ra, rb, rc)
memberdef.name: wesely_ra_rb

briefdescription.para: calculates the Ra and Rb term in the Wesely scheme

detaileddescription.para: References: Wesely, M. L. "Parameterization of surface resistances to gaseous dry deposition in regional-scale numerical models." Atmospheric environment 41 (2007): 52-63. https://doi.org/10.1016/0004-6981(89)90153-4

parameterdescription.para: output of quasi-laminar boundary layer resistance [s/m]

memberdef.argsstring: (spc, is_dust, is_seasalt, lucindex, a_radi, a_den, press, temp, ustar, rhb, w10, lowerbin, upperbin, vtsout, rc)
memberdef.name: aero_sfcrsii

briefdescription.para: computes the aerodynamic resistance of aerosols.

detaileddescription.para: References: Zhang, L., Gong, S., Padro, J., & Barrie, L. (2001). A size-segregated particle dry deposition scheme for an atmospheric aerosol module. Atmospheric environment., https://doi.org/10.1016/S1352-2310(00)00326-5
Emerson, E. W., et al. (2020). Revisiting particle dry deposition and its role in radiative effect estimates. PNAS, 117(42), 26076-26082. https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.2014761117

parameterdescription.para: mapping above II to the 15 drydep land use categories

memberdef.argsstring: (spc, is_seasalt, rhbl, rdry, rwet, diam, den, rc)
memberdef.name: new_diam_den

briefdescription.para: updates the diameter and density of non-dust aerosols

detaileddescription.para: References: Adapted from GEOS-Chem source code (GeosCore/drydep_mod.F90) ADUST_SFCRSII and AERO_SFCRSII functions

briefdescription.para: calculates the volume size distribution of sea-salt.

detaileddescription.para: This only has to be done once. We assume that sea-salt is the combination of a coarse mode and accumulation model log-normal distribution functions. The resulting arrays are: DMID = diameter of bin and SALT_V = dV/dln(D) [in um3].
References: Adapted from GEOS-Chem source code (GeosCore/drydep_mod.F90) INIT_WEIGHTSS function

parameterdescription.para: lowest edge of sea salt radius [um]
uppest edge of sea sakt radius [um]

briefdescription.para: calculates the molecular diffusivity [m2/s] in air for a gas X of molecular weight XM [kg] at temperature TK [K] and pressure PRESS [Pa].

memberdef.argsstring: (temp, pressu, xmw, ustar, obk, zo, thik, is_gas, ra, rb, rc)
memberdef.name: wesely_ra_rb

briefdescription.para: calculates the Ra and Rb term in the Wesely scheme

detaileddescription.para: References: Wesely, M. L. "Parameterization of surface resistances to gaseous dry deposition in regional-scale numerical models." Atmospheric environment 41 (2007): 52-63. https://doi.org/10.1016/0004-6981(89)90153-4

parameterdescription.para: output of quasi-laminar boundary layer resistance [s/m]

detaileddescription.para: This group contains modules and functions for calculating dry deposition of atmospheric species in the CATChem system.
CATChem core data types and routines.
